query_normalized: cross-dataset consistency of alpha peak frequency estimates in resting-state eeg neuroscience
rationale: This paper focuses on EEG biometrics for person recognition using spectral coherence and power-spectrum features, not on alpha peak frequency estimates or their cross-dataset consistency. While it uses resting-state EEG (same general domain), it does not measure the specific variable (alpha peak frequency) or address the core question (cross-dataset consistency/reproducibility), making it a distinct construct sharing only homonym keywords like "EEG" and "resting-state."
